summary: Microorganisms are the primary constituent of dental plaque because they form its essential structural elements through adhesion and production of extracellular materials; other components such as food particles or leukocytes contribute less significantly by comparison. Thus, \boxed{\text{microorganisms}} represent the majority within plaque structures.
